items.title: MFA is not enabled for Root Account
items.description: Ensure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for the AWS root account.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "MFA is not enabled for Root Account". CLI: `aws iam list-mfa-devices` and check account summary for AccountMFAEnabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for the AWS root account. Sign in as root → Security credentials → Assign MFA (hardware FIDO or virtual MFA). Store recovery codes offline.

items.title: MFA is not enabled for IAM Users
items.description: Ensure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for all AWS IAM users with AWS Console access.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "MFA is not enabled for IAM Users". CLI: `aws iam list-mfa-devices` and check account summary for AccountMFAEnabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for all AWS IAM users with AWS Console access. Enforce MFA via IAM policy; remove console access for users without MFA registered.

items.title: Multiple Access Keys exists for IAM Users
items.description: Detects when a canary token access key has been used
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Multiple Access Keys exists for IAM Users".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Detects when a canary token access key has been used Rotate keys/credentials on a 90-day schedule. Prefer IAM roles over long-lived access keys.

items.title: Cross-Account Access Lacks External ID and MFA
items.description: Ensure cross-account IAM roles use either MFA or external IDs to secure the access to AWS resources.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Cross-Account Access Lacks External ID and MFA". CLI: `aws iam list-mfa-devices` and check account summary for AccountMFAEnabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure cross-account IAM roles use either MFA or external IDs to secure the access to AWS resources. Enforce MFA via IAM policy; remove console access for users without MFA registered.

items.title: Lack of Access Key Rotation
items.description: Ensure AWS IAM access keys are rotated on a periodic basis as a security best practice (90 Days).
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Lack of Access Key Rotation".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS IAM access keys are rotated on a periodic basis as a security best practice (90 Days). Rotate keys/credentials on a 90-day schedule. Prefer IAM roles over long-lived access keys.

items.title: Password Expiration is Disabled
items.description: Ensure AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user passwords are reset before expiration (90 Days).
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Password Expiration is Disabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user passwords are reset before expiration (90 Days). Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Weak Password Policy ( AWS Default password policy) is set for the AWS account
items.description: Ensure AWS account has an IAM strong password policy in use
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Weak Password Policy ( AWS Default password policy) is set for the AWS account".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS account has an IAM strong password policy in use Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Weak IAM Server Certificate in use
items.description: Ensure that all your SSL/TLS certificates are using either 2048 or 4096 bit RSA keys instead of 1024-bit keys.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Weak IAM Server Certificate in use".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that all your SSL/TLS certificates are using either 2048 or 4096 bit RSA keys instead of 1024-bit keys. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: IAM Role Policy are Too Permissive
items.description: Ensure AWS IAM policies attached to IAM roles are not too permissive.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "IAM Role Policy are Too Permissive".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS IAM policies attached to IAM roles are not too permissive. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: IAM Access Analyzer is not Enabled
items.description: Ensure that IAM Access Analyzer feature is enabled to maintain access security to your AWS resources.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "IAM Access Analyzer is not Enabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that IAM Access Analyzer feature is enabled to maintain access security to your AWS resources. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Pre-Heartbleed Server Certificates
items.description: Ensure that your server certificates are not vulnerable to Heartbleed security bug.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Pre-Heartbleed Server Certificates".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that your server certificates are not vulnerable to Heartbleed security bug. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Root Account Access Keys Present
items.description: Ensure that your AWS account (root) is not using access keys as a security best practice.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Root Account Access Keys Present".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that your AWS account (root) is not using access keys as a security best practice. Rotate keys/credentials on a 90-day schedule. Prefer IAM roles over long-lived access keys.

items.title: Lack of SSH Public Keys Rotation
items.description: Ensure IAM SSH public keys are rotated on a periodic basis to adhere to AWS security best practices.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Lack of SSH Public Keys Rotation". Check resource policies, ACLs, and security groups for Principal "*" or 0.0.0.0/0.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure IAM SSH public keys are rotated on a periodic basis to adhere to AWS security best practices. Remove public access blocks bypass, tighten bucket policies / security groups, and require private endpoints or VPC-only access.

items.title: SSL/TLS Certificate is about to Expire
items.description: Ensure SSL/TLS certificates are renewed before their expiration.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "SSL/TLS Certificate is about to Expire".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure SSL/TLS certificates are renewed before their expiration. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Security Challenge Question not Enabled
items.description: Ensure security challenge questions are enabled and configured to improve the security of your AWS account.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Security Challenge Question not Enabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure security challenge questions are enabled and configured to improve the security of your AWS account. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Security Contact Information is not Registered
items.description: Ensure alternate contacts are set to improve the security of your AWS account.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Security Contact Information is not Registered".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure alternate contacts are set to improve the security of your AWS account. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: AWS Multi-Account are managed centrally via Identity Federation or AWS Organization
items.description: Set up, organize and manage your AWS accounts for optimal security and manageability.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "AWS Multi-Account are managed centrally via Identity Federation or AWS Organization".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Set up, organize and manage your AWS accounts for optimal security and manageability. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Root Account Recently used
items.description: Ensure root account credentials have not been used recently to access your AWS account.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → IAM. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in IAM. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Root Account Recently used".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure root account credentials have not been used recently to access your AWS account. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

sections.title: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) , Elastic Blob Storage (EBS), Elastic Load Balancer V2 (ELBv2)

items.title: Overbroad Ingress Rules for Security Groups
items.description: Ensure no EC2 security group allows unrestricted inbound access to any uncommon ports.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Overbroad Ingress Rules for Security Groups".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure no EC2 security group allows unrestricted inbound access to any uncommon ports. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: EC2 Instance Termination Protection is not Enabled
items.description: Ensure Termination Protection feature is enabled for EC2 instances that are not part of ASGs.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "EC2 Instance Termination Protection is not Enabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Termination Protection feature is enabled for EC2 instances that are not part of ASGs. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: AMIs are Publicaly Shared
items.description: Ensure your Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) are not accessible to all AWS accounts.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "AMIs are Publicaly Shared". Check resource policies, ACLs, and security groups for Principal "*" or 0.0.0.0/0.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure your Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) are not accessible to all AWS accounts. Remove public access blocks bypass, tighten bucket policies / security groups, and require private endpoints or VPC-only access.

items.title: Golden/Approved AMIs not in Use
items.description: Ensure all AWS EC2 instances are launched from approved AMIs.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Golden/Approved AMIs not in Use".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure all AWS EC2 instances are launched from approved AMIs. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Amazon EBS Snapshots are Publicly Accessible
items.description: Ensure that your Amazon EBS volume snapshots are not accessible to all AWS accounts.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Amazon EBS Snapshots are Publicly Accessible". Check resource policies, ACLs, and security groups for Principal "*" or 0.0.0.0/0.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that your Amazon EBS volume snapshots are not accessible to all AWS accounts. Remove public access blocks bypass, tighten bucket policies / security groups, and require private endpoints or VPC-only access.

items.title: Amazon EBS Volumes Encryption is not enabled
items.description: Ensure that existing Elastic Block Store (EBS) attached volumes are encrypted to meet security and compliance requirements.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Amazon EBS Volumes Encryption is not enabled". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that existing Elastic Block Store (EBS) attached volumes are encrypted to meet security and compliance requirements. Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: Amazon EBS Snapshots Encryption is not enabled
items.description: Ensure Amazon EBS snapshots are encrypted to meet security and compliance requirements.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Amazon EBS Snapshots Encryption is not enabled". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Amazon EBS snapshots are encrypted to meet security and compliance requirements. Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for EBS Volume Encryption
items.description: Ensure EBS volumes are encrypted with KMS CMKs in order to have full control over data encryption and decryption.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for EBS Volume Encryption". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure EBS volumes are encrypted with KMS CMKs in order to have full control over data encryption and decryption. Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: Weak Cryptographic Controls for ELB
items.description: Ensure AWS Application Load Balancers (ALBs) are using the latest predefined security policy.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Weak Cryptographic Controls for ELB".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS Application Load Balancers (ALBs) are using the latest predefined security policy. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Load Balancer is not Integrated with AWS WAF
items.description: Ensure that WAF ACL is integrated with Elastic Load Balancer
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Load Balancer is not Integrated with AWS WAF".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that WAF ACL is integrated with Elastic Load Balancer Attach AWS WAF web ACL with managed rule groups. Block known bad inputs and rate-limit abusive clients.

items.title: ELB uses In-secure protocols
items.description: Ensure that your Application Load Balancer (ALB) listeners are using a secure protocol such as HTTPS.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "ELB uses In-secure protocols".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that your Application Load Balancer (ALB) listeners are using a secure protocol such as HTTPS. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: ELB Deletion Protection Disabled
items.description: Ensure Deletion Protection feature is enabled for your AWS load balancers to follow security best practices.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "ELB Deletion Protection Disabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Deletion Protection feature is enabled for your AWS load balancers to follow security best practices. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Access logging disabled for ELB
items.description: Ensure access logging is enabled for your AWS ALBs to follow security best practices.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → EC2 / VPC → Security Groups. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Elastic Compute Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Access logging disabled for ELB".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure access logging is enabled for your AWS ALBs to follow security best practices. Enable logging to a dedicated, encrypted, immutable store. Integrate with CloudWatch/SIEM alerts.

items.title: VPC Flow Logs Disabled
items.description: Ensure Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Flow Logs feature is enabled in all applicable AWS regions.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → VPC. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Virtual Private Cloud. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "VPC Flow Logs Disabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Flow Logs feature is enabled in all applicable AWS regions. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Overbroad S3 Access Control
items.description: Ensure that your AWS S3 buckets are not publicly exposed to the Internet.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Overbroad S3 Access Control".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that your AWS S3 buckets are not publicly exposed to the Internet. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Cross-Account Access for S3 Buckets
items.description: Ensure Amazon S3 buckets do not allow unknown cross account access via bucket policies.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Cross-Account Access for S3 Buckets".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Amazon S3 buckets do not allow unknown cross account access via bucket policies. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Server-Side Encryption is not Enabled for S3
items.description: Ensure AWS S3 buckets enforce Server-Side Encryption (SSE)
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Server-Side Encryption is not Enabled for S3". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS S3 buckets enforce Server-Side Encryption (SSE) Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for S3 Buckets Encryption
items.description: Ensure that Amazon S3 buckets are encrypted with customer-provided AWS KMS CMKs
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for S3 Buckets Encryption". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that Amazon S3 buckets are encrypted with customer-provided AWS KMS CMKs Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: Versioning and Multi-Factor Delete is not Enabled on S3 buckets
items.description: Ensure AWS S3 buckets have the MFA Delete feature enabled. Ensure AWS S3 object versioning is enabled for an additional level of data protection.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Versioning and Multi-Factor Delete is not Enabled on S3 buckets".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS S3 buckets have the MFA Delete feature enabled. Ensure AWS S3 object versioning is enabled for an additional level of data protection. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: Access Logging Disabled for S3
items.description: Ensure AWS S3 buckets have server access logging enabled to track access requests.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Access Logging Disabled for S3".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS S3 buckets have server access logging enabled to track access requests. Enable logging to a dedicated, encrypted, immutable store. Integrate with CloudWatch/SIEM alerts.

items.title: Secure Transport is Not Enabled on S3
items.description: Ensure AWS S3 buckets enforce SSL to secure data in transit
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket permissions / Block Public Access. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Simple Storage Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Secure Transport is Not Enabled on S3".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure AWS S3 buckets enforce SSL to secure data in transit Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: CloudTrail Log Encryption Disabled
items.description: Ensure your AWS CloudTrail logs are encrypted using AWS KMS–Managed Keys (SSE-KMS).
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudTrail. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Cloud Trail. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "CloudTrail Log Encryption Disabled". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure your AWS CloudTrail logs are encrypted using AWS KMS–Managed Keys (SSE-KMS). Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for CloudTrail Encryption
items.description: Ensure that KMS master keys are used for CloudTrail Encryption
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudTrail. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Cloud Trail. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "KMS Customer Master Keys is not used for CloudTrail Encryption". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that KMS master keys are used for CloudTrail Encryption Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: CloudTrail Log File Validation is Disabled
items.description: Ensure your AWS CloudTrail trails have log file integrity validation enabled.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudTrail. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Cloud Trail. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "CloudTrail Log File Validation is Disabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure your AWS CloudTrail trails have log file integrity validation enabled. Enable logging to a dedicated, encrypted, immutable store. Integrate with CloudWatch/SIEM alerts.

items.title: CloudTrail is not integrated with CloudWatch
items.description: Ensure CloudTrail event monitoring with CloudWatch is enabled.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudTrail. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Cloud Trail. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "CloudTrail is not integrated with CloudWatch".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure CloudTrail event monitoring with CloudWatch is enabled. Enable logging to a dedicated, encrypted, immutable store. Integrate with CloudWatch/SIEM alerts.

items.title: No Security Incident Alarm exist for AWS Services
items.description: Ensure that Security Incident Alarms are created in CloudWatch
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudWatch → Alarms. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in CloudWatch. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "No Security Incident Alarm exist for AWS Services".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that Security Incident Alarms are created in CloudWatch Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: RDS Database instance are Publicly accessible
items.description: Ensure RDS database instances are not publicly accessible and prone to security risks.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → RDS → Databases. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Relational Database Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "RDS Database instance are Publicly accessible". Check resource policies, ACLs, and security groups for Principal "*" or 0.0.0.0/0.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure RDS database instances are not publicly accessible and prone to security risks. Remove public access blocks bypass, tighten bucket policies / security groups, and require private endpoints or VPC-only access.

items.title: Deletion protection is not Enabled for RDS Instance
items.description: Ensure Deletion Protection feature is enabled for your AWS RDS database instances.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → RDS → Databases. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Relational Database Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Deletion protection is not Enabled for RDS Instance".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure Deletion Protection feature is enabled for your AWS RDS database instances. Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: RDS Automated Backup is not enabled
items.description: Ensure that Automated Backups are created for the RDS Instances
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → RDS → Databases. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Relational Database Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "RDS Automated Backup is not enabled".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that Automated Backups are created for the RDS Instances Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: RDS Instance Encryption is not Enabled
items.description: Ensure AWS RDS instances are encrypted to meet security and compliance requirements.
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → RDS → Databases. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in Relational Database Service. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "RDS Instance Encryption is not Enabled". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.

items.title: Older Version of ElastiCache Engine in Use
items.description: Ensure that you are not using an older version of elasticache and use the latest version that is available
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → ElastiCache. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in ElastiCache. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Older Version of ElastiCache Engine in Use".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that you are not using an older version of elasticache and use the latest version that is available Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.

items.title: ElastiCache Redis cluster In-Transit and At-rest Encryption not enabled
items.description: Ensure that redis clusters data In-transit and At-rest encryptions are enabled
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → ElastiCache. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in ElastiCache. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "ElastiCache Redis cluster In-Transit and At-rest Encryption not enabled". Verify encryption-at-rest flags on the resource and default encryption settings.
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that redis clusters data In-transit and At-rest encryptions are enabled Enable encryption at rest with AWS KMS CMK. Re-encrypt existing volumes/snapshots where supported.

items.title: Access logging disabled for CloudFront
items.description: Ensure that access logging is enabled for Cloudfront
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudFront. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in CloudFront. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "Access logging disabled for CloudFront".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that access logging is enabled for Cloudfront Enable logging to a dedicated, encrypted, immutable store. Integrate with CloudWatch/SIEM alerts.

items.title: WAF is Not Enabled in CloudFront
items.description: Ensure that WAF is enabled for all the available Cloudfront
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudFront. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in CloudFront. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "WAF is Not Enabled in CloudFront".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that WAF is enabled for all the available Cloudfront Attach AWS WAF web ACL with managed rule groups. Block known bad inputs and rate-limit abusive clients.

items.title: TLSv1.0 Supported by CloudFront Distribution
items.description: Ensure that you are using latest TLS version for all Cloudfront
items.how_to_find: Console: AWS Console → CloudFront. Review resources matching this finding. CLI: Use AWS CLI to list and describe resources in CloudFront. Automated: Run Prowler or ScoutSuite and filter findings for "TLSv1.0 Supported by CloudFront Distribution".
items.how_to_fix: Remediation goal: Ensure that you are using latest TLS version for all Cloudfront Apply least-privilege IAM policies, enable service-specific security defaults, and validate with AWS Config rules.
